positions.name: iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Core broad market exposure to S&P 500. IE-domiciled accumulating structure eliminates 30% WHT on distributions — structurally optimal vehicle for a Lebanese resident. Held as the portfolio anchor — largest position by design, targeting 36% of NAV. Currently underweight at 29.35%; $250 ADD is overdue (recommended April 11, unexecuted). Post-trade target weight: ~31.4%, with full gap closure to 36% via Tranche 3 deployment.
invalidation_conditions: Structural shift away from US equities as the dominant long-term growth engine
A superior WHT-efficient broad market vehicle becomes available

positions.name: Amazon.com Inc
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy entered during Liberation Day tariff shock. Core thesis: AWS cloud dominance (~33% global cloud market share), advertising revenue growing at double-digits, and AI infrastructure investment (Bedrock, Trainium custom silicon) make AMZN a compounding platform business. Tariff risk is concentrated in retail/logistics; AWS as a domestic cloud business is materially insulated from import tariff exposure. No dividend means 0% WHT. Currently up +11.42% from entry — the portfolio's leading performer. Portfolio is up 11.4% on cost while AMZN is down ~7.5% YTD from 2026 highs, confirming the entry quality.
invalidation_conditions: AWS loses meaningful cloud market share to Azure or GCP on a sustained basis
Regulatory breakup that separates AWS from the retail/advertising business
Sustained margin compression across all segments (retail, advertising, AND cloud simultaneously)

positions.name: SPDR Gold Shares
positions.thesis: Macro hedge against dollar weakness, geopolitical risk, and inflationary pressure. Gold has no cash flows so carries zero WHT exposure despite US domicile. Held as a portfolio stabilizer and return enhancer. Up +53% on a trailing 12-month basis — the YTD asset class leader. April 7 ceasefire may reduce the near-term geopolitical safe-haven bid but structural gold drivers remain intact: central bank gold accumulation at multi-decade highs, weaponisation of USD reserves driving non-US central banks to diversify, US debt trajectory, and real rates below long-run equilibrium.
invalidation_conditions: Sustained USD strengthening cycle driven by structurally higher real rates that persist above inflation
Major reversal in central bank gold buying trend (multiple major central banks becoming net sellers)
Risk-off environment fully resolves AND portfolio has better risk-adjusted deployment opportunity in equities

positions.name: Mastercard Inc
positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend — confirmed $0.22 withheld on $0.73 April dividend. Annualised WHT drag <$1 at current position size. Immaterial.
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy during Liberation Day volatility. Thesis: payments network duopoly (MA + Visa) with near-monopoly economics, asset-light model generating high ROE, global consumer spending volume exposure, and pricing power. Currently -2.84% from entry — weakness is macro-driven (tariff uncertainty, consumer sentiment softness) not a thesis breakdown. Dividend yield <1% means WHT drag is under $1/year at current size. Designated Tranche 3 co-target alongside CSPX if S&P drops an additional 5-7% — averaging down would materially improve cost basis.
invalidation_conditions: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, open banking) gaining scale at the expense of card networks
Regulatory caps on interchange fees in core US or European markets
Sustained global consumer spending contraction lasting multiple quarters

positions.name: NVIDIA Corporation
positions.thesis: AI infrastructure buildout driving unprecedented GPU demand. NVDA holds dominant position in AI training and inference hardware; CUDA ecosystem creates deep switching costs that no competitor has credibly overcome at scale. H100/B200 upgrade cycle is intact. Currently +3.66% from entry but underperforming Nasdaq peers — index +4.1% vs NVDA +1% this week signals relative momentum weakness. Key risk: China export restrictions and growing investor scrutiny of hyperscaler AI capex sustainability. Thesis requires monitoring Q1 earnings commentary from major hyperscalers (AMZN, MSFT, GOOGL) on AI infrastructure spend trajectory. Hold conviction; do not add until technical momentum resumes.
invalidation_conditions: AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia) takes meaningful and sustained share in AI training workloads
AI capex cycle reverses — hyperscalers visibly cut or guide down infrastructure spend across multiple quarters
Export restrictions to China expand materially beyond current guidance, reducing total addressable market by >15%

positions.name: ASML Holding NV
positions.wht_exposure: 15% Dutch WHT assumed (treaty rate). ACTION REQUIRED: verify on next IBKR tax document whether 15% (NL treaty) or 30% (US NRA flat) is actually being applied. If 30%, evaluate switching to AMS-listed shares to eliminate US NRA withholding.
positions.thesis: Monopoly supplier of EUV lithography systems — no credible competitor exists for leading-edge chip manufacturing. Every advanced fab (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) is ASML-dependent for sub-7nm production. Currently +22% YTD and +5.81% from entry — portfolio's strongest risk-adjusted performer. Q1 2026 earnings on April 15 are the key near-term catalyst: consensus EUR 10.21B revenue / EUR 7.72 EPS. TSMC strong Q1 results (reported this week) are a positive read-through for ASML order visibility. A beat with intact 2026/2027 order guidance would strongly reinforce the thesis. A guidance cut on EUV orders would be a thesis-level event triggering a position review.
invalidation_conditions: China develops a credible domestic EUV alternative — assessed as a 5-10 year horizon risk, not near-term
Semiconductor capex supercycle ends and fab customers cancel or significantly delay EUV orders at scale
Dutch government export restrictions expand materially beyond current ASML guidance
Q1 2026 earnings guidance cut: EUV order intake falls materially below 2025 run-rate

positions.name: iShares USD Treasury Bond 0-3 Month UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Cash equivalent / capital preservation layer yielding ~4%+ T-bill rate with 0% effective WHT. Replaced SGOV (which carried 30% WHT on distributions) — a materially superior structure for Lebanese residents. US Treasury interest paid to an Irish-domiciled fund qualifies for the portfolio interest exemption from NRA withholding. Near-zero duration risk (0-3M). Marginally overweight at 16.28% vs. 15% target — within tolerance. As the Fed approaches its rate cut cycle, opportunity cost of holding a cash equivalent rises; flag for gradual trimming over subsequent rebalancing passes.
invalidation_conditions: Fed cuts rates aggressively (>100bps) — opportunity cost of T-bill equivalent rises significantly vs equities
A better WHT-efficient fixed income vehicle with superior risk-adjusted return is identified
Portfolio NAV grows large enough (>$10K) that maintaining a 15% cash equivalent layer imposes unacceptable drag on long-term returns
